footer.tagline: Estimate cosmetic and perfume manufacture dates from supported batch-code formats, with clear shelf-life guidance and limitations.
footer.rights: © {year} {name}. All rights reserved. Estimates are informational only and not a guarantee of product safety.

hero.title: Check Cosmetic & Perfume Batch Codes
hero.subtitle: Estimate the manufacture date and age of supported cosmetics, then review typical unopened shelf life and PAO guidance.

home.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about decoding cosmetic and perfume batch codes.

form.batchCodeHint: Not the long barcode (EAN) under the black stripes. The batch code is a short, separate stamp of letters and numbers on the base, crimp or label.
form.eanTitle: The barcode is not the batch code
form.eanBody: The long number under the black stripes is the barcode (EAN). It only names the product — every bottle of the same item shares it, so it can't tell you a date. The batch code is a separate, short stamp (letters and numbers) printed or embossed on the base, crimp or label. That's the one to enter.
form.eanWrongSub: Not this — identical on every unit
form.eanRightSub: Enter this — a short date stamp
form.errorNoCode: Enter the batch code from your product.

brandPage.metaTitle: {name} Batch Code Checker — How Old Is Your Product?
brandPage.metaDescription: Enter your {name} batch code to read its manufacture date in seconds. See how old the product is and how much of its typical shelf life is left.
brandPage.blurb: Read a {name} batch code to estimate its manufacture date and age. Shelf-life guidance is an estimate, and a batch code alone cannot prove authenticity.
brandPage.intro: Enter the short {name} batch code below to estimate its manufacture date and product age. This is not a manufacturer-issued expiry or authenticity check.
brandPage.whereHeading: Where to find the code on {name} packaging
brandPage.freshnessPara: Once we read the manufacture date, we estimate freshness using {name}'s typical {shelf}-month shelf life for {category} products. Remember that the period-after-opening (about {pao} months) becomes the limit once you start using the product.
brandPage.aliasesPara: You'll sometimes see this tool called a {name} batch code decoder, {name} batch code checker, or {name} batch code calculator — they all do the same thing: read the production code and tell you how fresh your {name} {category} is.
brandPage.printsDateNote: {name} prints the manufacture or expiry date directly on the packaging, so you usually don't need a batch code.
brandPage.decoderGuideBody: {name} is associated with {group}. Where its code matches the observed group format, the decoder reads the date pattern; regional or contract-manufactured products may use another format.
brandPage.decoderGuideLink: Review the {name} code format and limitations

lorealFamily.heading: What to know about {name} and L'Oréal batch codes
lorealFamily.portfolio: {name} is listed in L'Oréal Groupe's brand portfolio. Group ownership does not guarantee that every factory, market or older product uses the same batch-code format.
lorealFamily.format: Where the entered code matches L'Oréal's observed group coding, the checker reads its production month and year for you. Enter the code above to get the date — there are no manual steps to follow.
lorealFamily.precision: This format identifies a month, not an exact day. Any day displayed in the result is a mid-month estimate. A printed expiry or best-before date on the product always takes priority.
lorealFamily.authenticity: A code that decodes successfully cannot prove the product is genuine because a code can be copied. Use the result as one consistency check alongside the seller, packaging, seals and print quality.
lorealFamily.officialSource: Confirm the brand in L'Oréal Groupe's official portfolio

storageTip.skincare: Keep it out of direct sunlight and away from heat, and close caps tightly. Bathrooms get humid, so a cool, dry drawer is better — especially for active ingredients like vitamin C, retinol or acids, which oxidise faster once exposed to air and light.
storageTip.makeup: Store it away from heat and moisture and keep liquids and mascara tightly closed. Sharpen pencils, wash brushes and avoid adding water or saliva, which introduce bacteria and shorten how long the product stays safe to use.
storageTip.perfume: Keep the bottle in its box, away from light, heat and humidity. Avoid storing it in the bathroom — heat and steam break down the fragrance oils faster than age alone, dulling the scent and shifting the colour.
storageTip.haircare: Keep bottles closed and out of direct shower heat and steam when not in use, and store them somewhere cool and dry. Water left inside pumps or caps can encourage mould over time.
storageTip.suncare: Store it below 25°C, out of direct sun, and never leave it in a hot car or on the beach for hours — heat degrades UV filters, so an expired or overheated sunscreen protects less than a fresh one.
storageTip.generic: Store it somewhere cool and dry, out of direct sunlight, and keep the container tightly closed between uses.

brandFaq.q_where: Where is the batch code on {name} products?
brandFaq.a_where: Look for a short stamped, embossed or ink-jet code — usually on the base of the bottle or jar, the crimp at the end of a tube, or the bottom or flap of the box. On {name} {noun} it is separate from the barcode and typically 3–8 characters of letters and numbers.
brandFaq.q_barcode: What's the difference between the {name} batch code and the barcode?
brandFaq.a_barcode: The barcode is the long striped EAN/UPC number used at checkout and is the same for every unit of a product. The {name} batch code is a short production code that changes with each manufacturing run and encodes when the item was made — that's the one this tool decodes.
brandFaq.q_expired: How do I know if my {name} product is expired?
brandFaq.a_expired: Enter your {name} batch code above to get the manufacture date, then compare it to the typical {shelf}-month unopened shelf life. If more time has passed than that — or more than about {pao} months since you opened it — treat the product as expired, especially if the texture, colour or smell has changed.
brandFaq.q_unopened: How long do unopened {name} products last?
brandFaq.a_unopened: Unopened {name} {noun} typically stays good for about {shelf} months from the manufacture date when stored properly. This is an industry estimate — {name} does not print a hard expiry date on most items, which is why the batch code matters.
brandFaq.q_pao: How long does {name} last after opening (PAO)?
brandFaq.a_pao: Once opened, {name} {noun} should generally be used within about {pao} months — this is the Period After Opening, shown as the open-jar symbol (e.g. {pao}M) on the packaging. After you open a product, the PAO becomes the limit even if the batch itself is still fresh.
brandFaq.q_paoSymbol: What does the open-jar (PAO) symbol on {name} mean?
brandFaq.a_paoSymbol: The little open-jar icon with a number and an "M" (for months) is the Period After Opening. For most {name} {noun} it's around {pao}M, meaning roughly {pao} months of safe use from the day you first open it. The batch code tells you how fresh it was before opening; the PAO tells you how long it lasts after.
brandFaq.q_afterExpiry: Can I still use {name} after the expiration date?
brandFaq.a_afterExpiry: Using {name} {noun} shortly past its estimated date is usually a cosmetic-quality issue rather than a safety one — actives lose potency, textures separate and fragrances fade. Stop using it if you notice a change in smell, colour or consistency, and never use eye or lip products that are old, as bacteria risk is higher.
brandFaq.q_store: How should I store {name} {noun} to keep it fresh?
brandFaq.q_fake: Can a batch code tell me if my {name} is fake?
brandFaq.a_fake: A batch code decoder reads the production date — on its own it can't prove a product is genuine. But a {name} code that won't decode, or a date that doesn't match when or where you bought it, can be a red flag. For authenticity, also check the packaging, seals and font, and buy from authorised {group} retailers.
brandFaq.q_accuracy: How accurate is this {name} batch code decoder?
brandFaq.a_accuracy: We read the manufacture date using {decoder}. Where {group} publishes a known code scheme the result is precise to the month or day; where the brand doesn't confirm a public scheme it's a best-estimate reading. The date is the manufacture date, not a guaranteed expiry — shelf-life figures are typical industry averages.
brandFaq.q_free: Is the {name} batch code checker free?
brandFaq.a_free: Yes — decoding {name} batch codes on Cosmetics Batch is completely free and requires no sign-up. Checks are processed securely on our server; IP addresses are not included in our batch-code quality dataset.

homeFaq.a1: A batch code is a short code printed on cosmetic and perfume packaging that identifies the production run. Decoding it reveals when the product was manufactured.
homeFaq.q2: Is this batch code checker free?
homeFaq.a2: Yes. Cosmetics Batch is completely free, requires no account, and processes codes securely on the server.
homeFaq.a3: We use manufacturer-specific algorithms where available and honest confidence levels elsewhere. Shelf-life estimates are typical values, not guarantees of safety.
homeFaq.q4: Where do I find my batch code?
homeFaq.a4: It's usually a short stamped or embossed code on the base of a bottle, the crimp of a tube, or the bottom of the box — separate from the barcode.
homeFaq.q5: Does an expired product mean it's unsafe?
homeFaq.a5: Not necessarily. It means the product is past its typical shelf life and may have reduced performance. Eye products and anything that smells or looks off should be discarded.
homeFaq.q6: What does the open-jar (PAO) symbol mean?
homeFaq.a6: The little open-jar icon with a number like 12M or 24M is the Period After Opening — how many months a product stays good once you first open it. After opening, this becomes the limit even if the batch code date is recent.
homeFaq.q7: What's the difference between shelf life and expiration date?
homeFaq.a7: Most cosmetics don't print an expiry date. The batch code gives the manufacture date, and we estimate the expiration by adding the product's typical unopened shelf life — usually 24–36 months for makeup and skincare, and 3–5 years for perfume.
homeFaq.a8: Most fragrances last 3 to 5 years from the manufacture date, and often longer if stored cool and away from light. Perfume rarely becomes unsafe, but oxidation slowly changes the scent — the top notes fade first.
homeFaq.q9: Why can't my batch code be decoded?
homeFaq.a9: Some brands change their coding over time, use region-specific formats, or don't encode the date publicly. Double-check you entered the stamped batch code (not the barcode), or try selecting the parent company instead of the sub-brand.
homeFaq.q10: Do you store my batch codes or personal data?
homeFaq.a10: No account is required and IP addresses are not included in our batch-code quality dataset. We may retain the code, brand, result, language, time and country-level code to improve decoder accuracy; see the Privacy Policy for details.

result.invalidBody: “{code}” doesn’t match {brand}’s batch-code format. Check that you typed it exactly as printed on the packaging — letters and numbers only, no spaces — and that it’s the batch code, not the barcode or a price/marketing reference.
result.meaningFresh: This product is fresh and well within its shelf life.
result.meaningGood: This product is still good, with plenty of shelf life remaining.
result.meaningUseSoon: This product is nearing the end of its shelf life — use it up soon.
result.meaningExpired: This product has passed its shelf life and may no longer be at its best quality.
result.meaningUnknown: We could not estimate the freshness for this code.

whereCode.title: Where is the batch code?
whereCode.subtitle: It is a short separate stamp — here is where it usually hides on different packs.

alt.perfume: Batch code highlighted on a perfume box
alt.tube: Batch code highlighted on a cosmetic tube crimp
alt.lipstick: Batch code highlighted on a lipstick base

whereCode.cardCta: Where to find the batch code on a {type} — read the guide

calvin-klein.where1: On the bottom of the box, and on the sticker under the bottle.
calvin-klein.where2: On the flatter CK One bottles the code can be printed directly on the glass base.
calvin-klein.faq1q: How do I decode a Calvin Klein batch code?
calvin-klein.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Calvin Klein code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
calvin-klein.faq2q: Can the batch code tell me if my CK is real?
calvin-klein.faq2a: It can catch a bad fake: an impossible day number, or codes that disagree between box and bottle. It cannot confirm a good one, because a code copied from a genuine bottle decodes perfectly.

carolina-herrera.where1: On the base of the box, and the underside of the bottle.
carolina-herrera.where2: On the Good Girl stiletto bottle the code sits on the sole of the heel.
carolina-herrera.faq1q: How do I check a Carolina Herrera batch code?
carolina-herrera.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Carolina Herrera code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
carolina-herrera.faq2a: We can't honestly put a number on it: nobody publishes reliable counterfeiting rates, and any site that quotes one is guessing. What is worth doing either way are the checks a code cannot do — compare the box and bottle codes, weigh the bottle, and look at the fineness of the spray. A clean decode is necessary but nowhere near sufficient.

cerave.where1: Tubes and cleansers: pressed into the crimp at the sealed end of the tube.
cerave.where2: Pump bottles (SA Cleanser, moisturising lotion): inkjet-printed on the back label or on the base.
cerave.faq1q: How do I check the shelf life of a CeraVe product?
cerave.faq1a: CeraVe is made by L'Oréal and carries the group's batch code, which holds the manufacture date. Enter it in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read the date for you, then reckon on around 36 months unopened. Once opened, the open-jar symbol takes over — 12 months for most of the range.
cerave.faq2q: Is there an expiry date on CeraVe?
cerave.faq2a: Sometimes, and the rule behind it is worth knowing: under EU cosmetics law a product that keeps for more than 30 months doesn't need a printed expiry date, and carries the open-jar (PAO) symbol instead. So a pack showing only a symbol is not missing anything — and a pack that does print a date is telling you it is labelled for 30 months or less. Where a printed date exists, trust it: it is the manufacturer's own guarantee and it beats anything a decoder can estimate.
cerave.faq3q: How can I tell if CeraVe is counterfeit?
cerave.faq3a: CeraVe is widely counterfeited on marketplaces. The batch code is a first filter — a code that does not fit L'Oréal's shape is a bad sign — but it cannot confirm a genuine product, since a real code can be copied onto a fake tube. Compare the texture of the plastic, the print quality of the small text, and buy from a pharmacy or an authorised seller.

chanel.where1: Fragrance: laser-etched into the glass on the base of the bottle, and printed on the bottom flap of the box. The etching has no ink — tilt the bottle so light catches it from the side.
chanel.where2: Makeup and skincare: on the base of the compact or jar, in very small grey type.
chanel.faq1q: Does Chanel have an official batch code system?
chanel.faq1a: No. Chanel has never published one, and anyone claiming to know the official Chanel cipher is guessing. What is verifiable is that current Chanel products print a readable production date inside the code, and that is what we decode — reported as a medium-confidence read precisely because there is no published scheme to check it against.
chanel.faq2q: I can't find any code on my Chanel bottle.
chanel.faq2a: It is almost certainly laser-etched into the glass base rather than printed. Etching is invisible under a direct light and appears when light rakes across it — turn the bottle slowly under a lamp, or photograph the base with flash and zoom into the picture.
chanel.faq3q: How do I check the shelf life of Chanel foundation?
chanel.faq3a: Decode the code on the base for the manufacture date, then apply the open-jar symbol — typically 12M for a liquid foundation. Whichever runs out first, the unopened shelf life or the months since you opened it, is the real deadline.

clinique.where1: Skincare bottles and jars: embossed on the base.
clinique.where2: Makeup: the back of the compact or the base of the case.
clinique.faq1q: How do I read a Clinique batch code?
clinique.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Clinique code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
clinique.faq2q: How long does Clinique skincare last?
clinique.faq2a: Around 36 months unopened from the manufacture date, and 12 months after opening for most of the range. The open-jar symbol on the pack is the number that binds once you break the seal.

creed.where1: On the base of the bottle, and on the bottom of the box — usually printed in black on the white base sticker.
creed.where2: On the older, heavier flacons the code can be stamped rather than printed; angle it against the light.
creed.faq1q: How do I read a Creed batch code?
creed.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Creed code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
creed.faq2q: Can the code tell me if my Green Irish Tweed is genuine?
creed.faq2a: Partly. A code that doesn't fit Creed's format at all is a straightforward tell. But a copied code decodes cleanly, so a valid code is not proof — weigh the bottle, look at the spray and the box printing, and buy from an authorised retailer.
creed.faq3q: Does an older Creed mean a better Creed?
creed.faq3a: For the heavier, resinous compositions an older bottle can genuinely be preferable, because reformulations have changed several classics — and the batch code is the only way to establish which formula you are buying. For the lighter, citrus-led Creeds, age is mostly oxidation and no benefit at all.

dior.where1: Fragrance: on the base of the outer box, and on the underside of the bottle — a small printed sticker, or laser-etched into the glass on the larger bottles.
dior.where2: Makeup: on the base of the compact, the bottom of the lipstick case, or the crimp of a tube.
dior.where3: Skincare: stamped on the base of the jar, next to the open-jar symbol.
dior.faq1q: How do I check whether my Dior perfume is genuine?
dior.faq1a: Start with the codes: the one on the box and the one on the bottle should match, because both are applied on the same filling line minutes apart. Then decode — a month or year that predates the fragrance's launch is a real warning. But understand the limit: a counterfeiter who copies a genuine code produces a code that decodes perfectly. The batch code can expose a fake; it cannot certify a real one.
dior.faq2q: What does a Dior batch code look like?
dior.faq2a: A short code — a handful of characters, mixing letters and digits — printed or etched on the base of the bottle and on the box. It holds the production date, but not written out as a date: enter it in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read it for you. One limitation is worth knowing: the code doesn't record the decade, so a much older bottle can produce the same reading as a recent one. The bottle style and the fragrance's launch date are what settle it. Some vintage Diors carry a plain string of digits instead.
dior.faq3q: Where is the batch code on Dior lipstick?
dior.faq3a: On the flat base of the case. Twist the bullet fully down first — the code is stamped into the base and is easy to miss against the metallic finish.
dior.faq4q: Can I check Dior Sauvage, Dior Homme and Miss Dior here?
dior.faq4a: Yes. Choose Dior and enter the batch code from Sauvage, Dior Homme or Homme Intense, or Miss Dior. The checker estimates the manufacture date from the Dior code. Compare the box and bottle codes too, but remember that a valid date cannot prove the fragrance is genuine.

dunhill.where1: On the base of the box and the underside of the bottle, printed in black on the white base sticker.
dunhill.faq1q: How do I read a Dunhill batch code?
dunhill.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Dunhill code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
dunhill.faq2q: How do I check a Dunhill fragrance is authentic?
dunhill.faq2a: Compare the box and bottle codes, then decode. A code that doesn't fit the format at all, or codes that disagree between box and bottle, are real warnings. A clean decode is not proof — copied codes decode too.

escada.where1: Modern eau de parfum and eau de toilette: on the clear sticker under the glass bottle, printed as a short number on the same line as the "EAU DE PARFUM VAPORISATEUR" text.
escada.where2: Outer carton: on an end flap. The sticker and carton codes are printed together at the factory and should read the same — if they don't, the bottle and box were paired somewhere else.
escada.where3: Older Escada parfum minis (the Margaretha Ley era, distributed by Escada Beauté) stamp a short letter-plus-number lot into the metal base plate instead. That vintage format is not the code Coty uses today and won't decode here.
escada.faq1q: How do I read the batch code on an Escada perfume?
escada.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Escada code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
escada.faq2q: My Escada bottle has a code like "H 0100" stamped on the base — why won't it decode?
escada.faq2a: That is an older Escada parfum from before the current Coty coding; the Margaretha Ley-era minis stamp a letter-and-number lot into the metal base rather than printing the code Coty uses today. Those vintage lots don't follow the format this tool reads, so it can't date them. It is not a sign the bottle is fake — the factory simply used a different system.
escada.faq3a: Fragrance has no hard expiry, but it does change. An unopened Escada bottle kept cool and dark holds its character for around five years; once opened, the bright fruity top notes fade first. A bottle that has darkened or turned sharp and sour has oxidised regardless of what the code says.
escada.faq4q: Can the batch code prove my Escada is genuine?
escada.faq4a: No. A code that doesn't fit Coty's format, or that dates to before the scent launched, is a real warning sign. But a well-formed code copied onto a counterfeit decodes just as cleanly, so a valid date proves the code is real, not the perfume. Buying from an authorised retailer remains the stronger check.

estee-lauder.where1: Skincare: embossed on the base of the jar or bottle — pressed into the material, so it needs a raking light rather than a direct one.
estee-lauder.where2: Fragrance and makeup: on the base of the box, and on the underside of the product.
estee-lauder.faq1q: How do I read an Estée Lauder batch code?
estee-lauder.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Estée Lauder code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
estee-lauder.faq2q: How do I check the shelf life if I threw away the box?
estee-lauder.faq2a: The code is on the product itself, not only the carton — on the base of the jar or bottle, usually embossed. Decode it for the manufacture date, then apply the open-jar symbol from the day you opened it. Whichever deadline comes first is the real one.
estee-lauder.faq3q: Do Clinique, MAC and La Mer use the same code?
estee-lauder.faq3a: Yes — the same plants and the same plant/month/year format. Only the plant letter differs, and it carries no date information.

fenty-beauty.where1: Foundation and liquid products: printed on the base of the bottle, or where the label wraps around the back.
fenty-beauty.where2: Compacts, powders and gloss: on the back of the case or the base of the tube.
fenty-beauty.faq1q: How do I check whether Fenty Beauty is authentic?
fenty-beauty.faq1a: Decode the batch code — Fenty is an LVMH brand and prints a production date in the code. A code that produces an impossible date, or no date at all, is a warning. But a copied code decodes cleanly, so use it alongside the usual checks: print quality on the packaging, the feel of the applicator, and buying from Sephora or Fenty directly.
fenty-beauty.faq2q: The barcode on my Fenty product won't scan. Is it fake?
fenty-beauty.faq2a: Not necessarily — a barcode that won't scan is usually a print or lighting problem, and in any case the barcode carries no date and no authenticity information. It is the batch code, the short irregular stamp, that tells you when the product was made.
fenty-beauty.faq3q: How long does Fenty foundation last?
fenty-beauty.faq3a: Roughly 12 months after opening and about 36 months unopened from the manufacture date. Whichever comes first is the deadline.

garnier.where1: Serums and day creams: on the base of the bottle or the crimp of the tube.
garnier.where2: SPF and Ambre Solaire: printed on the back label, usually with an explicit expiry date as well.
garnier.where3: Sachets and single-use masks: on the back of the sachet, in very small type near the seam.
garnier.faq1q: Where do I find the expiry date on a Garnier cream or serum?
garnier.faq1a: Often there is none printed, and that is a labelling rule rather than an omission: under EU cosmetics law a product that keeps for more than 30 months doesn't need a printed expiry date, and carries the open-jar (PAO) symbol instead. The short code on the tube crimp or bottle base is the batch code, and it holds the manufacture date rather than an expiry — enter it in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read the date for you. Count forward about 36 months for an unopened product, and use the open-jar symbol once it has been opened.
garnier.faq2q: Where is the date on Garnier sun cream?
garnier.faq2a: Sun protection is the exception — it normally does carry a printed expiry date, because the SPF claim is a safety claim. Use the printed date rather than a decoded one, and replace any bottle that has spent a summer in the heat.
garnier.faq3q: How do I know my Garnier product is real?
garnier.faq3a: Check that the code fits L'Oréal's format — Garnier is a L'Oréal brand — and that it decodes to a plausible date. That rules out crude fakes. It cannot rule out a good one, because a copied code decodes perfectly.

giorgio-armani-beauty.where1: Fragrance (Acqua di Giò, Armani Code): on the bottom of the box and the base sticker of the bottle.
giorgio-armani-beauty.where2: Makeup: on the base of the case or the back of the compact.
giorgio-armani-beauty.faq1q: How do I decode an Armani Code batch number?
giorgio-armani-beauty.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Armani code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
giorgio-armani-beauty.faq2q: How long does Acqua di Giò last unopened?
giorgio-armani-beauty.faq2a: Three to five years is normal for an eau de toilette, longer for the eau de parfum. Fragrance is the most forgiving category there is, provided the bottle is kept out of light and heat.

guerlain.where1: Fragrance: on the base of the box, and on the underside of the bottle — often printed on a small sticker.
guerlain.where2: Makeup and skincare: stamped on the base of the compact or jar.
guerlain.faq1q: How do I check a Guerlain fragrance's production date?
guerlain.faq1a: Guerlain is an LVMH house and prints the production date inside the batch code rather than on the pack. Enter the code in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read the date for you, then reckon on three to five years of unopened life for an eau de parfum kept out of light and heat.
guerlain.faq2q: My old Guerlain code won't decode.
guerlain.faq2a: Older and region-specific codes are sometimes internal alphanumeric references with no date in them at all. Those cannot be decoded by anyone. Judge the bottle by its condition and the smell of the juice instead.

hugo-boss.where1: On the base of the outer box, and on the clear sticker under the bottle.
hugo-boss.where2: The two codes should match — they are printed minutes apart on the same line.
hugo-boss.faq1q: How do I check a Hugo Boss fragrance batch code?
hugo-boss.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Hugo Boss code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
hugo-boss.faq2q: The box and bottle codes are different. Is it fake?
hugo-boss.faq2a: It means the box and the bottle were paired somewhere other than the factory. That happens with counterfeits, and also with legitimately repackaged, returned or grey-market stock. Either way, the pairing you are holding is not the one the factory shipped.

jean-paul-gaultier.where1: On the base of the box, and on the underside of the torso-shaped bottle.
jean-paul-gaultier.where2: On the metal-cased editions the code is frequently on the box only, or stamped faintly into the base of the can.
jean-paul-gaultier.faq1q: How do I read a Jean Paul Gaultier batch code?
jean-paul-gaultier.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Jean Paul Gaultier code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
jean-paul-gaultier.faq2q: There is no code on my Le Male bottle.
jean-paul-gaultier.faq2a: On the metal-sleeved editions the code is often only on the box, or stamped so faintly into the base that it needs raking light to see. Check the carton first — and if you have both, confirm that they match.

kenzo-parfums.where1: On the base of the outer box, and on the underside of the bottle — printed on the base sticker or stamped into the glass.
kenzo-parfums.where2: On the Kenzo World and Flower bottles the code can sit on the flat underside where the label folds under.
kenzo-parfums.faq1q: How do I check a Kenzo fragrance by its batch code?
kenzo-parfums.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Kenzo code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
kenzo-parfums.faq2q: Do Kenzo codes repeat every ten years?
kenzo-parfums.faq2a: Yes, and this is a genuine limitation rather than a quirk. The code doesn't record the decade, so a bottle from ten years ago can produce exactly the same reading as a recent one — the evidence is identical either way. The decoder assumes the recent reading; if the bottle and packaging look like they belong to an earlier era, they probably do.
kenzo-parfums.faq3q: Can the code prove a Kenzo fragrance is authentic?
kenzo-parfums.faq3a: It can catch a fake — an impossible day, or a mismatch between the box code and the bottle code, which no genuine filling line produces. It cannot certify a genuine one, because a copied code decodes cleanly.

kerastase.where1: Bottles and masks: inkjet-printed on the back of the bottle, near the base, or stamped on the bottom of the jar.
kerastase.where2: Tubes and travel sizes: pressed into the crimp.
kerastase.faq1q: How do I find the shelf life of a Kérastase mask?
kerastase.faq1a: Kérastase is L'Oréal Professionnel and carries the group's batch code, which holds the manufacture date. Enter it in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read the date for you. An unopened mask is typically good for around 30–36 months from that date; once opened, the open-jar symbol applies — usually 12M.
kerastase.faq2q: How do I check a Kérastase product is authentic?
kerastase.faq2a: Kérastase is a common target for counterfeits and for diverted salon stock. Decode the code: it should fit L'Oréal's format and produce a plausible date. It won't catch a copied code, so also look at the quality of the printing, the weight and finish of the cap, and whether the seller is an authorised salon or retailer.
kerastase.faq3q: The product was bought abroad — does the shelf life change?
kerastase.faq3a: No. The formula's shelf life is a property of the product, not the market. What does change with a hot climate is how fast it actually degrades: heat shortens real-world life well below the printed one.

kiehls.where1: Jars and bottles: stamped on the base, often embossed rather than printed — tilt it under a light.
kiehls.where2: Tubes and eye creams: pressed into the crimp.
kiehls.faq1q: How do I check the shelf life of Kiehl's eye cream?
kiehls.faq1a: Enter the code from the base in the checker at the top of this page — Kiehl's is L'Oréal, and we read the group's code for you. Unopened, reckon on around 36 months; once opened, the open-jar symbol — typically 12M for an eye cream, which is the number that will bind first.
kiehls.faq2q: The code on my Kiehl's jar is invisible.
kiehls.faq2a: It is usually embossed into the base rather than inked. Embossed codes disappear under a direct light and appear when light rakes across them from the side — turn the jar slowly against a lamp.

la-roche-posay.where1: Tubes: pressed into the crimp at the sealed end.
la-roche-posay.where2: Bottles and Anthelios sunscreen: printed on the back label, usually alongside a genuine expiry date.
la-roche-posay.faq1q: Where is the expiry date on La Roche-Posay?
la-roche-posay.faq1a: The pharmacy lines usually print a real expiry date on the carton or the tube — use it. The short code beside it is the batch code, and holds the manufacture date instead — enter it in the checker at the top of this page if that is all you can find.
la-roche-posay.faq2q: How long does Anthelios sunscreen last?
la-roche-posay.faq2a: Up to three years unopened if it is kept cool, and about 12 months once opened. Sunscreen is the one product where the printed date genuinely matters, because degraded filters mean less protection than the label promises.
la-roche-posay.faq3a: A code that fits L'Oréal's format and decodes to a plausible date rules out the crude fakes. It cannot certify a good one. Pharmacy purchase remains the strongest guarantee for this range.

lancome.where1: Fragrance: on the base of the box, and on the underside of the bottle.
lancome.where2: Skincare and foundation: embossed or printed on the base of the jar, the tube crimp, or the bottle.
lancome.faq1q: How do I read a Lancôme batch code?
lancome.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Lancôme code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
lancome.faq2q: How long does unopened Lancôme skincare last?
lancome.faq2a: Around three years from manufacture, less for products built on unstable actives. Decode before buying from a discounter — old stock is genuine, but it may have very little unopened life left.

loreal.where1: Anywhere the group's products are stamped: tube crimps, bottle bases, jar bottoms and carton flaps.
loreal.where2: Use this page when a sub-brand isn't listed separately — the whole group shares one code format.
loreal.faq1q: Which brands share the L'Oréal batch code?
loreal.faq1a: All of them: L'Oréal Paris, Maybelline, Garnier, NYX, Essie, Lancôme, Kiehl's, YSL Beauty, Giorgio Armani, Urban Decay, CeraVe, La Roche-Posay, Vichy, SkinCeuticals, Kérastase, Redken, Biotherm and the rest. They come off the same plants, so they carry the same format.
loreal.faq2q: What does a L'Oréal code look like?
loreal.faq2a: A short string of letters and digits, stamped or inkjet-printed rather than written out as a date. Only part of it carries the production date; the rest identifies the factory and the production run, and says nothing about shelf life. Enter the whole code in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read the date for you.
loreal.faq3q: My code has no letter in it.
loreal.faq3a: Then it isn't this format. Some recently acquired brands, and some regional contract manufacturers, print a plain Julian date instead — the checker falls back to reading a date embedded in the digits.

loreal-paris.where1: Creams and jars: stamped on the base of the jar, or on the underside of the lid.
loreal-paris.where2: Tubes: pressed into the crimp at the sealed end.
loreal-paris.where3: Bottles (shampoo, serum, foundation): inkjet-printed on the back label or the base.
loreal-paris.faq1q: How do I check the shelf life of a L'Oréal Paris cream?
loreal-paris.faq1a: Usually there is no expiry date to find, and that is a labelling rule rather than an omission: under EU cosmetics law a product that keeps for more than 30 months doesn't need a printed expiry date, and carries the open-jar (PAO) symbol instead. What is printed is a batch code holding the manufacture date. Enter it in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read that date for you. From it, an unopened cream is typically good for around 36 months, and the open-jar symbol governs how long it lasts once you start using it.
loreal-paris.faq2q: Where is the manufacture date printed on L'Oréal products?
loreal-paris.faq2a: It isn't printed as a date — it is encoded in the batch code, which is the short irregular stamp on the crimp, the base or the back label. The long number under the barcode stripes is the EAN and contains no date at all.
loreal-paris.faq3q: How can I check a L'Oréal product is authentic?
loreal-paris.faq3a: Decode the code and see whether it fits L'Oréal's format at all — a code the format doesn't recognise, or one decoding to an impossible date, is a genuine warning sign. But a copied code decodes cleanly, so this test can catch a fake and cannot certify a real one. Compare the print quality of the small text on the packaging, and buy from an authorised retailer.

mac-cosmetics.where1: Lipstick: stamped into the flat base of the bullet case. Twist the bullet fully down so the case sits flat.
mac-cosmetics.where2: Compacts, blushes and palettes: on the back of the case — and on some products printed on the metal pan, only visible as the powder is used up.
mac-cosmetics.where3: Foundation bottles: on the base, or where the label wraps around the back.
mac-cosmetics.faq1q: How do I check the expiry date of MAC foundation?
mac-cosmetics.faq1a: Usually there is none to find, and that is a labelling rule rather than an omission: under EU cosmetics law a product that keeps for more than 30 months doesn't need a printed expiry date, and carries the open-jar (PAO) symbol instead. What MAC prints instead is a short batch code holding the manufacture date. Enter it in the checker at the top of this page, then apply the open-jar symbol: liquid foundation is typically 6–12 months once opened, and around 36 months unopened. The earlier deadline wins.
mac-cosmetics.faq2q: What does a MAC batch code look like?
mac-cosmetics.faq2a: Three characters, such as B23. MAC belongs to the Estée Lauder group, so the last character is the year digit and the middle one is the month — where A, B and C mean October, November and December.
mac-cosmetics.faq3q: How can I tell whether my MAC lipstick is genuine?
mac-cosmetics.faq3a: The code should be three or four characters and decode to a plausible date; a code that doesn't fit that shape is a warning. But a copied code decodes perfectly, so also judge the weight of the case, the click of the closure and the print of the small text — and buy from an authorised counter.

maybelline.where1: Mascara: printed on the base of the tube, or vertically where the label wraps around.
maybelline.where2: Foundation bottles: on the base, or on the back label.
maybelline.where3: Palettes and compacts: on the back of the case.
maybelline.faq1q: How old is my Maybelline mascara?
maybelline.faq1a: Enter the code from the base in the checker at the top of this page — Maybelline is L'Oréal, and we read the group's code for you. But for mascara the manufacture date rarely binds: the three-to-six-month clock that starts the day you open the tube almost always runs out first.
maybelline.faq2q: Where is the batch code on Maybelline products?
maybelline.faq2a: On the base of the tube or bottle, or the back of a palette. It is the short irregular code — not the long barcode number.

montblanc.where1: On the base of the box, and on the underside of the bottle — printed in black on the white base sticker.
montblanc.where2: Montblanc codes are among the easier fragrance codes to read without a magnifier.
montblanc.faq1q: How do I check a Montblanc Legend by its code?
montblanc.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Montblanc code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
montblanc.faq2q: Is there a serial number I can look up?
montblanc.faq2a: No. A batch code identifies a production run, not an individual bottle, and there is no public database to look it up in. What it gives you is the date — which is the useful part.
montblanc.faq3q: The date I get looks wrong.
montblanc.faq3a: On a minority of Inter Parfums codes part of the code identifies a production line rather than a date, which can push the reading out. If the date looks implausible for the bottle, treat the day as approximate and weigh it against the bottle's own apparent age.

nivea.where1: Tubes (hand cream, face wash): stamped into the crimp — the flat sealed end opposite the cap.
nivea.where2: The classic blue Creme tin: pressed into the metal base, with no ink at all. Tilt it under a light; a straight-on light makes it vanish.
nivea.where3: Bottles and sun care: inkjet-printed on the back label or the shoulder of the bottle.
nivea.faq1q: How do I find the manufacture date on NIVEA products?
nivea.faq1a: NIVEA (Beiersdorf) records the production week rather than an exact day, so the reading places the product within about seven days — ample for a body lotion. Enter the code in the checker at the top of this page and we'll work the date out for you.
nivea.faq2q: Does NIVEA sun cream have an expiry date?
nivea.faq2a: Sun protection normally carries a printed expiry date, because the SPF claim is treated as a safety claim. Use the printed date rather than a decoded estimate — and treat a bottle that spent a summer in a hot car as finished regardless of what the date says.
nivea.faq3q: There is no code visible on my NIVEA tin.
nivea.faq3a: It is embossed into the metal base rather than printed, which makes it nearly invisible under direct light. Hold the tin so a lamp catches the base from the side and the characters will appear.

paco-rabanne.where1: On the bottom of the box, and on the underside of the bottle. On the heavier bottles — the 1 Million ingot, the Invictus trophy — the code is often stamped into the metal or glass rather than printed on a sticker.
paco-rabanne.where2: Tilt the bottle so light catches the stamp from the side; a direct light makes it disappear.
paco-rabanne.faq1q: How do I decode an Invictus or 1 Million batch code?
paco-rabanne.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Paco Rabanne code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
paco-rabanne.faq2q: Can the code prove my Paco Rabanne is genuine?
paco-rabanne.faq2a: It can catch a fake and cannot certify a real one. Codes that disagree between box and bottle, or a date that could not exist, are strong warnings — but a code copied from a genuine bottle decodes perfectly. Weigh the bottle and check the spray pattern too.
paco-rabanne.faq3q: Is Rabanne the same brand as Paco Rabanne?
paco-rabanne.faq3a: Yes — the house dropped 'Paco' from its name in 2023. Older stock carries the previous branding, and the code format did not change.

redken.where1: Bottles: inkjet-printed on the back label or the base of the bottle.
redken.where2: Powders and aerosols: stamped on the base of the can.
redken.faq1q: How do I check the shelf life of a Redken product?
redken.faq1a: Redken is a L'Oréal brand and carries the group's batch code, which holds the manufacture date — enter it in the checker at the top of this page and we'll read it for you. Haircare is typically good for around 36 months unopened and 12 months after opening.
redken.faq2q: Does the barcode tell me the shelf life?
redken.faq2a: No. The barcode is the EAN — it identifies the product for sale and is identical on every bottle. Only the batch code, the short irregular stamp, carries a date.

roberto-cavalli.where1: On the bottom of the box and the base of the bottle — printed, or on the heavier flacons stamped into the glass.
roberto-cavalli.where2: Look for a short code of four digits; the longer number under the barcode stripes is not it.
roberto-cavalli.faq1q: How do I check Roberto Cavalli perfume for authenticity?
roberto-cavalli.faq1a: Cavalli fragrance is made by Coty, and the code carries the production date. Compare the code on the box with the code on the bottle first: a mismatch is the single strongest signal available to a buyer. Then decode it; a date that cannot exist is a clear fake. A code that decodes cleanly, though, is not proof of authenticity — copied codes decode too.
roberto-cavalli.faq2q: Where is the serial number on a Roberto Cavalli bottle?
roberto-cavalli.faq2a: There isn't one in the sense of a unique per-bottle serial. What is printed is a batch code — shared by every unit from the same production run — and it encodes the date, not an identity.

tom-ford-beauty.where1: Fragrance: on the base of the box, and etched or printed on the underside of the bottle.
tom-ford-beauty.where2: Foundation and eyeshadow palettes: stamped on the base of the compact or the bottle.
tom-ford-beauty.faq1q: How do I check a Tom Ford batch code?
tom-ford-beauty.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Tom Ford code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
tom-ford-beauty.faq2q: How do I date a Tom Ford eyeshadow palette?
tom-ford-beauty.faq2a: The code is on the base of the compact. Decode it for the manufacture date; a pressed powder is good for around 24 months after opening and considerably longer unopened, because a dry formula gives bacteria almost nothing to grow in.
tom-ford-beauty.faq3q: Is the batch code proof of authenticity?
tom-ford-beauty.faq3a: No. It can expose a crude fake — a code that doesn't fit the format, or decodes to an impossible month — but a copied code decodes cleanly. Tom Ford fragrance is heavily counterfeited; weigh the bottle, check the atomiser spray, and buy from an authorised retailer.

too-faced.where1: Palettes and compacts: on the back of the case, sometimes under the plastic tray.
too-faced.where2: Liquid products: on the base of the bottle or the tube crimp.
too-faced.faq1q: How do I check a Too Faced palette's batch code?
too-faced.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Too Faced code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
too-faced.faq2q: My Too Faced code won't decode.
too-faced.faq2a: Some codes on Too Faced packaging are internal line markings, not date codes, and there is nothing in them to decode. Check the base and the back of the case for a second, shorter code — and if there genuinely isn't one, the open-jar symbol is your best guide.

vichy.where1: Tubes and Capital Soleil sunscreen: pressed into the crimp — the flat sealed end opposite the cap.
vichy.where2: Bottles and pumps (Mineral 89, Liftactiv): inkjet-printed on the base, or on the back label near the barcode.
vichy.where3: Cartons: on an end flap, usually beside the open-jar symbol. Samples and sachets often carry no code at all — only the carton they came in does.
vichy.faq1q: Where is the expiry date on Vichy products?
vichy.faq1a: Often there isn't one, and that is a labelling rule rather than an omission: under EU cosmetics law a product that keeps for more than 30 months doesn't need a printed expiry date, and carries the open-jar (PAO) symbol instead. Most skincare keeps longer than that. Where a printed date does exist — pharmacy lines often carry one — trust it: it beats any decoded estimate. If a short code is all you can find, that is the batch code, and it holds the manufacture date rather than the expiry. Enter it in the checker at the top of this page, then add the shelf life: roughly 36 months unopened for skincare, and follow the open-jar symbol once opened.
vichy.faq2q: How do I read the code on a Vichy product?
vichy.faq2a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Vichy code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
vichy.faq3q: Can the code prove my Vichy is genuine?
vichy.faq3a: Only partly. A code that doesn't fit L'Oréal's shape, or that decodes to a date before the product line existed, is a real warning. But a counterfeiter copying a code from a genuine tube produces a code that decodes perfectly — so a clean decode proves the code is well-formed, not that the cream is. For pharmacy skincare, buying from a pharmacy remains the stronger protection.
vichy.faq4a: Yes, and it matters more than for anything else in the range: degraded UV filters let through more radiation, so an out-of-date SPF 50 does not deliver SPF 50. Sunscreen normally carries a printed expiry date for exactly this reason — use it. And treat a bottle that spent a summer in a hot car as finished regardless of the date.

ysl-beauty.where1: Lipstick and makeup: stamped on the base of the case or the back of the compact.
ysl-beauty.where2: Fragrance: on the base of the box and the underside of the bottle.
ysl-beauty.faq1q: How do I check an Yves Saint Laurent batch code?
ysl-beauty.faq1a: Cosmetics Batch reads the production date from your Yves Saint Laurent code for you — enter it in the checker at the top of this page, with no manual decoding. The code holds the manufacture date, not an expiry: add the typical unopened shelf life to judge freshness, and once opened follow the open-jar (PAO) symbol.
ysl-beauty.faq2q: Why does my YSL code look like a Maybelline one?
ysl-beauty.faq2a: Because the same company makes both. The format follows the factory, not the price — a YSL lipstick and a Maybelline mascara come off L'Oréal lines and carry the same style of code.

zara.where1: On the bottom flap of the fragrance box, and on the base of the bottle.
zara.where2: The beauty range prints it on the underside of the case or the crimp of the tube.
zara.faq1q: Does Zara perfume have an expiry date?
zara.faq1a: Not a printed one, as a rule — like most fragrance it carries a batch code instead. Decode it for the manufacture date and reckon on three to five years of unopened life, which is normal for an eau de toilette kept away from light and heat.
zara.faq2a: Puig manufactures Zara's fragrance line, which is why the codes follow Puig's format — and part of why several Zara scents invite comparison with designer fragrances that Puig also makes.

contentPages.checkYourCodeBody: Enter the batch code from your product in the checker and it reads the manufacture date for you — no manual decoding needed. The code holds the production date, not the expiry.
contentPages.workedExamplesNote: Decoded live by the same engine that powers the checker — not transcribed by hand.
contentPages.brandsUsingNote: The batch code belongs to the manufacturer, not the label on the bottle — the brands below share the same production coding, so the checker reads them all.
contentPages.whichCode: Not sure which code on the pack is the batch code?
contentPages.formatsIntro: A batch code belongs to whoever manufactures the product, not to the name on the bottle — the same maker stamps the same style of code across many brands. Pick a manufacturer group below to see which brands it covers, then check any code in the tool.
contentPages.formatsIntro2: Each page below covers one manufacturer group: which brands it includes, where to find the batch code on the pack, and what the code can and can't tell you about production date and freshness.

checkPage.metaTitle: Batch Code Checker — How Old Is Your Product?
checkPage.metaDescription: Pick your brand and enter the batch code to read the manufacture date in seconds. See how old your cosmetic or perfume is and how much shelf life is left.
checkPage.intro: Select a brand and enter its batch code to read the manufacture date.
